hook: RT and Times of Israel — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 35 buckets, 61 articles, 8 distinct frames in today's coverage.

Frame: REPATRIATION_LOGISTICS (7 buckets). "First group of passengers disembarks from cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak
The Latest
- MV Hondius, the luxury cruise ship linked to…" — CBC World

Frame: PANDEMIC_ANXIETY_REFLEX (6 buckets). "WHO director tells public the deadly outbreak aboard the MV Hondius is 'not another COVID,' as health officials prepare for lengthy process…" — Times of Israel

Frame: WHO_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY (4 buckets). "To the people of Tenerife,
My name is Tedros, and I serve as the Director-General of the World Health Organization, the United Nations agen…" — AllAfrica

Frame: DOMESTIC_EXPOSURE_TRACKING (3 buckets). "Two people in Finland may have been exposed to hantavirus, according to the Finnish Institute for Health and Welfare (THL).
A woman diagnos…" — Yle Finland English

The paradox: RT (which typically contests Western institutional authority) and the Times of Israel (embedded in the Western health-security consensus) both amplify WHO's 'not another COVID' reassurance, with RT doing so even while branding the same vessel a 'plague ship'—sensati…

Silence as data: `china` didn't cover this — they covered Not present in this corpus despite China's acute institutional memory of SARS and COVID; likely dominated by Hormuz/Iran war economic coverage. instead.
**Mother Jones** (usa): Frames the outbreak primarily as a prediction-market event: nearly $3 million wagered on Polymarket on pandemic probability, a framing absent from every other bucket.
